The search button has an image with alt="" and aria-hidden="true", which is fine because it's decorative. But the button itself has no visible text and no aria-label. However, the button is of type="submit", so it has an implicit name from the value? No, submit buttons do not have a default value; the accessible name must come from elsewhere.
The logo-bar images have alt text, but some are missing? Let's see:

The feature cards have images with alt="" and aria-hidden="true". These are decorative icons, so alt="" is correct, and they are hidden, so it is fine. However, we must check if the alt attribute is present. It is present as alt="", so no issue.
The contact form has an input for last name with autocomplete="family-name" and aria-required="true", but we must check if the label is present.
The textarea in the contact form has a label, but we must check if the associated text is correct. It has "Message" and the required marker, so it is fine.
The button in the contact form: "Send your message" has visible text, so accessible name is present.

The newsletter form button: "Subscribe to newsletter" has visible text, so accessible name is present.
The contact form button: "Send your message" has visible text, so accessible name is present.
The social media links in the footer: we already checked, they have visible text.
The logo in the footer: <a href="/" aria-label="Luminary Studio — go to homepage"> has aria-label, so ok.
